architecture.summary: OSTB is a training-free framework that operates on frozen candidate VLMs by estimating a consensus sample-to-class transport plan via optimal transport over their combined outputs. The framework reads predictions (class probabilities and/or embeddings) from multiple VLMs, solves a self-adaptive optimal transport problem to infer a latent sample-class structure without modifying VLM parameters, and reuses this learned transport plan for three downstream tasks: (1) ranking VLMs by semantic and visual reliability, (2) fitting transport-conditioned visual classifiers for target adaptation, and (3) integrating predictions via reliability-weighted ensemble.

biblio.title: One Stone, Three Birds: Self-adaptive Optimal Transport for Multi-VLM Selection, Adaptation, and Ensembling

claims.statement: OSTB improves model ranking, adaptation stability, and ensemble robustness under heterogeneous candidate pools
The three deployment decisions (model selection, target adaptation, prediction integration) depend on the same latent object: a trustworthy sample-class structure in the target set
A consensus sample-to-class transport plan can be estimated without updating VLM parameters by leveraging complementary evidence from multiple VLMs
The learned transport structure is reused for model selection by ranking by combined semantic and visual reliability induced by the consensus plan
Target adaptation is obtained by fitting transport-conditioned visual classifiers
Ensembling is implemented through reliability-aware probabilistic integration using the learned transport plan
